title: etlingera rosea
intro: Etlingera rosea, also known as the wild ginger or hill galangal, is a type of plant species in the Zingiberaceae family that is native to the tropical regions of Asia. The medicinal properties of Etlingera rosea include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve nausea and vomiting, and reduce fever, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. In terms of herbal preparations, Etlingera rosea is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, and can also be dried and used in capsules or tinctures for its medicinal properties.
intro_study: According to "Journal of the Medical Association of Thailand = Chotmaihet thangphaet", etlingera rosea, or roselle, has been shown to decrease the excretion of various minerals in urine, including creatinine, uric acid, citrate, tartrate, calcium, sodium, potassium, and phosphate, which may be beneficial in preventing renal stone disease.
